leagueBriefing: Conference tournament Friday brought heavyweight statements and bracket chaos: LSU and South Carolina looked every bit like title threats, UCLA and Iowa survived tougher-than-expected stretches, and underdogs like Colorado and No. 12 seed Kansas State flipped quarterfinal scripts with late poise.

games.headline: LSU buries Oklahoma with 112-point explosion and sets SEC semifinal showdown
games.brief: LSU played with pace, pressure and shot-making from the jump, then turned a ranked matchup into a runaway. MiLaysia Fulwiley orchestrated the attack, Flau'jae Johnson took over after halftime, and the Tigers powered into a blockbuster semifinal against South Carolina.
bullets: LSU scored 112 points, the second-highest total in SEC Tournament history.
MiLaysia Fulwiley posted 22 points and 8 assists.
Flau'jae Johnson scored 21, with 16 coming in the second half, and passed 2,000 career points.
LSU shot 53% overall and 13-of-24 from 3.
Oklahoma's seven-game winning streak ended.
games.context: LSU now faces top-seeded South Carolina in one of the tournament's highest-stakes semifinal matchups.

games.headline: Duke's defense smothers Clemson to reach ACC semifinals
games.brief: Duke's identity showed up immediately in this quarterfinal, with disruptive defense setting the tone and a strong second-quarter surge creating enough separation. Clemson made a late push, but the Blue Devils had controlled too much of the game to let it slip.
bullets: Duke forced 11 turnovers and 4 shot-clock violations in the first half.
Toby Fournier had 17 points, 10 rebounds and 3 blocks.
Duke closed the second quarter on a 10-0 run to lead 38-19 at halftime.
Clemson cut a 25-point deficit to 13 late but got no closer.
Top-seeded Duke advanced to the ACC semifinals.
games.context: After losing to Clemson less than two weeks earlier, Duke answered with a postseason statement built on defense.

games.headline: UCLA shakes off slow start, extends streak and advances in Big Ten bracket
games.brief: Washington landed the first punch and had UCLA uncomfortable early, but the Bruins eventually found their rhythm through Lauren Betts and Kiki Rice. Once UCLA cleaned up the defensive glass and settled into half-court execution, the game tilted for good.
bullets: Lauren Betts scored 26 points with 8 rebounds.
Kiki Rice added 18 points and 6 assists.
UCLA won its 23rd straight game, matching a program record.
Washington led 22-12 in the second before UCLA answered with a 15-0 run.
The Bruins closed the third on a 7-0 spurt and pulled away in the fourth.
games.context: UCLA's comeback composure keeps a major title path alive and preserves elite momentum entering the semifinals.

games.headline: South Carolina overwhelms Kentucky to stay on track for fourth straight SEC title
games.brief: South Carolina looked fresher, faster and stronger from opening tip, forcing Kentucky into a chase it never escaped. Joyce Edwards and Madina Okot powered a dominant interior game, and Dawn Staley's group controlled the pace while keeping key legs fresh for the semifinal.
bullets: Joyce Edwards led South Carolina with 21 points.
Madina Okot posted 12 points and 13 rebounds.
The Gamecocks shot 53%, forced 19 turnovers and won paint scoring 46-24.
South Carolina led by 17 at halftime and pushed the margin to 24 in the second half.
Kentucky entered on a third game in three days.
games.context: South Carolina stayed in championship form and now gets LSU in a marquee SEC semifinal.

games.headline: Top-seeded TCU controls BYU to open title defense in the Big 12 tournament
games.brief: TCU played like the top seed from the opening quarter, building an early cushion and never letting BYU's momentum runs turn into real danger. The Horned Frogs were steady even before Olivia Miles found her scoring touch, then closed the final period with authority.
bullets: Marta Suarez scored 17 points with 8 rebounds for TCU.
TCU opened a 24-8 first-quarter lead and led by as many as 18 in the first half.
Olivia Miles finished with a 10-point, 10-rebound, 6-assist line.
BYU's Delaney Gibb scored 17, but the Cougars shot just one field goal in the first 5:30.
TCU advanced to the Big 12 semifinals to face Kansas State.
games.context: TCU's balanced win keeps its repeat hopes alive and reinforces its profile as a national contender.

games.headline: Ohio State survives late push from Minnesota to reach Big Ten semis
games.brief: This one felt like a grind from start to finish, and Ohio State made just enough winning plays in the final minutes to close it out. The Buckeyes defended key possessions late and got clutch shot-making from Jaloni Cambridge to avoid a quarterfinal upset.
bullets: Elsa Lemmila had 17 points and 11 rebounds for Ohio State.
Jaloni Cambridge added 17 points, 6 assists and the late jumper that sealed it.
Minnesota cut it to 58-55 with 1:32 left before Ohio State's final stop-and-score sequence.
Sophie Hart had 12 points and a career-high 19 rebounds for the Gophers.
Ohio State advanced to face UCLA in the semifinal.
games.context: Ohio State gets another crack at UCLA after losing the regular-season meeting in December.

games.headline: Fairfield rolls in MAAC quarterfinal to extend streak and keep three-peat bid alive
games.brief: Fairfield handled business like a seasoned tournament favorite, building an early edge and controlling the game across all four quarters. Kaety L'Amoreaux set the scoring tone, and the Stags never allowed Sacred Heart to threaten after halftime.
bullets: Kaety L'Amoreaux scored 21 points and Meghan Andersen added 16.
Fairfield won its ninth straight game.
The Stags led by double digits throughout the second half and by as many as 27.
Fairfield shot 46% while holding Sacred Heart to 35%.
Coach Carly Thibault-DuDonis reached 100 career wins, fastest in MAAC history.
games.context: Fairfield remains on track for a third straight MAAC title and NCAA Tournament berth.

games.headline: Louisville blasts Syracuse with early run and never looks back in ACC quarters
games.brief: Louisville seized control almost immediately and turned this quarterfinal into a one-sided track meet. Tajianna Roberts and a deep Cardinals rotation kept the pressure on, while Syracuse never recovered after losing key shot creation with an injured Dominique Darius.
bullets: Tajianna Roberts scored 17 points on 7-of-10 shooting.
Louisville made 56% from the field and held Syracuse to 34%.
The Cardinals opened on a 17-3 run and led by at least eight the rest of the game.
Syracuse shot 4-of-25 (16%) from 3-point range.
Second-seeded Louisville advanced to face North Carolina in the semifinal.
games.context: Louisville's form and efficiency put it in strong position for a deep ACC tournament push.

games.headline: Ole Miss shocks No. 5 Vanderbilt after building huge early cushion
games.brief: Ole Miss turned this quarterfinal upside down in the opening quarter, jumping out by 21 and then stretching the lead to 32 before surviving a wild late push. Latasha Lattimore and Cotie McMahon delivered a knockout offensive combo while Vanderbilt's late rally came too late.
bullets: Latasha Lattimore scored 28 and Cotie McMahon added 27.
Ole Miss opened with a 23-2 run and led by as many as 32 in the third.
Vanderbilt's Mikayla Blakes missed her first 12 shots and didn't make a field goal until late in the third.
Vanderbilt outscored Ole Miss 37-24 in the fourth after coach Shea Ralph was ejected.
Ole Miss earned its second win of the season over Vanderbilt.
games.context: The Rebels move on to face No. 4 Texas in another top-tier SEC semifinal test.

games.headline: Iowa extends streak to seven as Stuelke returns and powers quarterfinal win
games.brief: Iowa got a lift from Hannah Stuelke's return and quickly established control, then weathered Illinois' late push to secure a semifinal spot. The Hawkeyes' rebounding and first-half defense built enough margin to survive a final-minute squeeze.
bullets: Ava Heiden led Iowa with 16 points.
Hannah Stuelke returned from an elbow injury and scored 13 on 6-of-11 shooting.
Iowa won its seventh straight game.
Illinois played its third game in three nights and was led by Jasmine Brown-Haggard's 22 points.
The Hawkeyes led by as many as 22 in the second half before Illinois cut it late.
games.context: Iowa is two wins from another Big Ten tournament title and now gets a semifinal rematch with Michigan.

games.headline: West Virginia slams door late to put away Arizona State in Big 12 quarters
games.brief: Arizona State made one more push in the fourth, but West Virginia answered immediately with timely threes and transition points to restore control. Sydney Shaw's shooting and a sharp closing stretch sent the Mountaineers to the semifinal round.
bullets: Sydney Shaw hit five 3-pointers and scored 19 points.
West Virginia led 52-50 with 8:05 left before finishing on a decisive late run.
Gia Cooke added 16 points for the Mountaineers.
Arizona State went scoreless for more than six minutes in the fourth quarter.
No. 2 seed West Virginia advanced to the Big 12 semifinals.
games.context: West Virginia's poise in closing time continues to define its tournament profile under Mark Kellogg.

games.headline: North Carolina handles Virginia Tech behind Grant's career night
games.brief: North Carolina's offense found rhythm at the right moments and never let Virginia Tech fully climb back in once the Tar Heels seized control before halftime. Lanie Grant's breakout scoring and Nyla Harris' efficiency drove a complete quarterfinal performance.
bullets: Lanie Grant scored a career-high 21 points on 8-of-12 shooting.
Nyla Harris added 19 points and 10 rebounds.
UNC finished with four players in double figures and shot efficiently across the board.
Virginia Tech's Carleigh Wenzel scored 26, including 14-of-15 at the line.
North Carolina built a 13-point halftime lead and stayed in front by double digits most of the second half.
games.context: The Tar Heels advanced to face Louisville and remain one of the ACC's most balanced teams entering semifinal weekend.

games.headline: Texas sprints out early and cruises past Alabama into SEC semis
games.brief: Texas made this quarterfinal non-competitive in a hurry, exploding to an early lead and never giving Alabama room to settle. The Longhorns controlled both ends, and the lopsided margin let Vic Schaefer rest key pieces ahead of the semifinal.
bullets: Texas opened up a 40-12 lead early in the second quarter.
Jordan Lee scored 16 and Bryanna Preston added 13.
Madison Booker posted 11 points, 10 rebounds and 6 assists in just 18 minutes.
Texas forced 14 first-half turnovers and led by 33 at halftime.
The Longhorns won their sixth straight.
games.context: Texas advanced fresh into a semifinal against Ole Miss after limiting starter workloads in a comfortable win.

games.headline: Michigan's depth and rebounding wear down Oregon in Big Ten quarterfinal
games.brief: Michigan controlled this game with physical rebounding and second-chance pressure, gradually stretching the margin until it became a rout. Syla Swords and Olivia Olson anchored a balanced Wolverines attack that Oregon couldn't match over four quarters.
bullets: Syla Swords scored 17 and Olivia Olson added 16 for Michigan.
Michigan outrebounded Oregon 24-16 in the first half with a 12-4 edge in offensive boards.
Both teams struggled from deep: Oregon 3-of-19, Michigan 4-of-19.
Oregon committed 17 turnovers, eight more than Michigan.
The 22-point final margin was Michigan's largest lead of the night.
games.context: Michigan advanced to a high-stakes semifinal rematch with Iowa after splitting regular-season meetings.

games.headline: Colorado stuns Baylor with early three-point barrage and never relents
games.brief: Colorado grabbed control early and refused to give it back, turning a quarterfinal against a ranked seed into a composed upset. The Buffaloes' bench scoring and perimeter shooting set the tone, and Baylor could never shrink the gap to one-possession pressure.
bullets: Desiree Wooten scored 21 off the bench, hitting three 3-pointers.
Zyanna Walker added 19 as Colorado built a 20-10 first-quarter lead.
Colorado led by as many as 17 in the second quarter and stayed up double digits through the third.
No. 6 seed Colorado advanced to a conference semifinal for the first time in 11 years.
Baylor dropped its second straight game.
games.context: Colorado's upset run has now flipped the top half of the bracket and sets up a semifinal with West Virginia.

games.headline: recap text missing
recap text missing
recap text missing
recap text missing
No. 12 seed Kansas State rallies late again and stuns Oklahoma State at buzzer
games.brief: Kansas State's late-game magic held for a second straight day, this time in a one-point thriller. Jordan Speiser erupted after halftime, the Wildcats erased a fourth-quarter deficit, and Tess Heal's final free throw with 1.3 seconds left delivered a historic upset.
bullets: Freshman Jordan Speiser scored a career-high 21 points, including 18 in the second half.
Tess Heal made the go-ahead free throw with 1.3 seconds left.
Kansas State became the first No. 12 seed to reach the women's Big 12 semifinals.
The Wildcats used a 9-0 fourth-quarter run to retake control.
Stailee Heard led Oklahoma State with 18 points.
games.context: Kansas State's underdog run turned into one of the tournament's biggest stories and set up a semifinal against top-seeded TCU.

games.headline: recap text missing
recap text missing
recap text missing
recap text missing
recap text missing
Notre Dame torches NC State in second half behind Hidalgo's scoring tear
games.brief: Notre Dame took over this quarterfinal with shot creation and pace once the second half opened. Hannah Hidalgo kept piling up elite scoring nights, and the Irish turned a competitive first half into a double-digit route with a game-breaking third-quarter run.
bullets: Hannah Hidalgo scored 25 points, her sixth straight game with at least 25.
Iyana Moore added 20 points, 9 rebounds and 3 steals.
Notre Dame opened the third with a 16-1 run to break the game open.
NC State's Khamil Pierre posted 17 points and 14 rebounds (21st double-double this season).
The Irish have won seven straight and nine of their last 10.
games.context: Notre Dame's current form makes its semifinal with top-seeded Duke one of the ACC tournament's defining matchups.
